scenarios.narrative: A surge in enrollment overwhelms shared campus laboratories. Engineering, biology, and digital arts students are competing for limited lab time and equipment. The administration suggests a rigid top-down rationing system, but student groups argue that collaboration could unlock more efficient, fair access.

choices.text: Form a cross-disciplinary lab council with representatives from engineering, biology, and arts to co-design a shared schedule.
Create a pooled booking system where departments trade time slots based on project urgency.
Launch a student-led lab mentorship program to share techniques and reduce redundant equipment use.

scenarios.narrative: A student climate coalition pushes for fossil-fuel divestment, but the administration demands a unified, evidence-based plan. Environmental science students hold climate data, economics students model financial impacts, and communications majors craft narratives. Without collaboration, the movement risks fragmentation.

choices.text: Create a cross-disciplinary divestment task force combining climate data, economic modeling, and strategic messaging.
Host an open forum where students from multiple disciplines co-author a divestment white paper.
Partner with law and policy students to draft a formal proposal for the university’s investment committee.

scenarios.narrative: The university’s open-source learning platform is hit by a coordinated cyber attack. Malicious commits spread through the repository. Computer science students can identify the technical breach, but governance rules require consensus from contributors across departments.

choices.text: Assemble a rapid-response governance group of security, philosophy, and law students to define emergency rules.
Create a cross-department incident review board to approve a secure rollback and patch strategy.
Launch an open call for contributors from multiple disciplines to co-design a new governance protocol.

scenarios.narrative: A burst pipe floods a residence hall, displacing hundreds of students. Facilities proposes a rigid reassignment plan, but it ignores accessibility, mental health, and social support needs. Architecture, psychology, and public health students see an opportunity for a more humane, data-driven solution.

choices.text: Form a housing resilience team combining architecture, psychology, and public health to redesign temporary allocations.
Develop a collaborative mapping tool that prioritizes accessibility and social support in room assignments.
Coordinate peer support networks across departments to reduce stress during relocation.

scenarios.narrative: A campus blackout sparks interest in building a student-designed renewable microgrid. Electrical engineering students sketch technical layouts, environmental science students model emissions reductions, and business students calculate long-term savings. Without alignment, the proposal stalls.

choices.text: Create a microgrid design consortium that merges engineering feasibility, climate modeling, and financial analysis.
Host a cross-disciplinary hackathon to prototype microgrid scenarios and present them to facilities.
Partner with policy and communications students to craft a compelling case for pilot implementation.
